name: Lakes Loop
name_original: Circuit des lacs — boucle depuis Troncens
description: Hike the moderate 5.7-mile Circuit des lacs loop from Troncens, exploring hilly landscapes, woods, and the tranquil Lac de Monpardiac.
region_intro: Hiking around Troncens, located in the Gers department of the Occitanie region, offers diverse natural environments. The area features picturesque green hills, extensive woods, and varied landscapes, providing numerous paths for exploration. A significant natural highlight is the Lac du Lizet and its surrounding coteaux, integrated into a Natura 2000 site. On clear days, certain routes offer panoramas of the Pyrenees mountain range. Best hiking trails around Troncens The most popular hiking route is Lakes Loop, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through hilly landscapes and woods, passing tranquil lakes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Mazous Pond and Eiffel Bridges Loop, a moderate 9.3 miles (15.0 km) path. This trail features varied terrain and takes approximately 4 hours 7 minutes to complete. Local hikers also love the Marciac – Chiesa di Notre-Dame loop from Marciac, a 9.2 miles (14.9 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Troncens is defined by green hills, extensive woods, and the Lac du Lizet, with routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Troncens are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Troncens's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes can I expect on hikes around Troncens?
A: Hiking around Troncens offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll encounter picturesque green hills, extensive woods, and varied terrain. Many trails provide very beautiful views, especially from crests, and on clear days, you might even catch a panorama of the Pyrenees mountain range. The region also features the ecologically significant Lac du Lizet and its surrounding coteaux, part of a Natura 2000 site, as well as preserved bocage Gersois landscapes and rivers like Le Bouès.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Troncens area?
A: There are nearly 40 hiking trails around Troncens listed on komoot, offering a wide variety of options for different preferences and skill levels. These include routes through green hills, woodlands, and past tranquil lakes.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Troncens offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24663701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chiesa di Notre-Dame loop from Marciac&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that covers about 5 km. In total, there are 13 easy trails in the area, perfect for a relaxed stroll.

Q: What are some moderate hiking trails for those looking for a bit more challenge?
A: For those seeking a moderate challenge, Troncens has 24 such trails. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1189043178/mazous-pond-and-eiffel-bridges-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mazous Pond and Eiffel Bridges Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 15 km long and features varied terrain. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20524512" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marciac – Strutting loop from Marciac&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 10 km.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Troncens?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Troncens are circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1188151094/lakes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail winding through hilly landscapes and woods. Another example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10527572" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marciac – Chiesa di Notre-Dame loop from Marciac&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Troncens?
A: While hiking around Troncens, you can explore various natural features and landmarks. The Lac du Lizet and its surrounding hillsides are a significant natural highlight. You might also encounter the preserved bocage Gersois landscape. For cultural points of interest, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6047807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of the Assumption of Marciac&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6332190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Church of La Vache Rouge&lt;/a&gt;, both near some routes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Troncens?
A: The trails in Troncens are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied landscapes, the tranquility of the woods, and the beautiful views, especially those offering glimpses of the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Troncens?
A: Yes, the Troncens area offers several trails suitable for families. The easier routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24663701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chiesa di Notre-Dame loop from Marciac&lt;/a&gt;, are generally good choices for families with children. The region's diverse natural environments, including woods and lakes, provide engaging settings for younger explorers.

Q: Is Troncens a good place for dog-friendly hikes?
A: The natural environment of Troncens, with its extensive woods and open landscapes, is generally welcoming for dog walkers. Many trails are suitable for dogs, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific routes.

Q: Are there any local attractions or places to visit near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore local attractions. The nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6364610" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marciac&lt;/a&gt; is a notable settlement. For a taste of local agriculture, the Vergers Deguzan in Troncens is an organic orchard specializing in hazelnuts and apples, showcasing the region's agricultural heritage. You can find more information about it here: &lt;a href="https://www.coeursudouest-tourisme.com/en/touristic_sheet/vergers-deguzan-troncens-en-5316632/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vergers Deguzan&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Troncens?
A: Troncens offers pleasant hiking conditions through much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly beautiful, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, but the extensive woods provide shaded paths. On clear days, the views of the Pyrenees are best enjoyed when the weather is crisp.
tips: Wonderful resevoir set in a large open space with picnic benches for taking a break.
This is a beautiful lake surrounded by hills which give it a dramatic air.

faqs.question: Where does the Circuit des lacs — loop from Troncens trail start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The trail uniquely starts from behind the town hall of Troncens. Hikers need to locate and pass through a specific door near the post box to access the beginning of the path. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's generally assumed parking is available near the town hall in Troncens.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Circuit des lacs is classified as 'easy' and is accessible for a wide range of hikers, making it suitable for beginners and families. It features a moderate positive elevation gain with undulating sections rather than being entirely flat.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect a picturesque journey through varied landscapes, including beautiful hilly areas, valleys, and woods. The terrain is a mix of dirt tracks and asphalt roads, passing by fields of crops. A highlight is the serene and untamed natural environment around Lac de Monpardiac. You might also spot various water birds and enjoy views of the surrounding valleys and lakes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the tranquil Lac de Monpardiac, where picnic tables are available for a break. You'll also pass by the Cabournieu Reservoir and enjoy diverse natural settings with fields of crops and views of surrounding valleys.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this loop?
faqs.answer: The loop typically takes about 3 hours to complete on foot. It covers a distance of approximately 8.79 km.

faqs.question: Is the Circuit des lacs — loop from Troncens trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific leash rules are not detailed, outdoor trails in France are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Given the varied landscapes and presence of lakes, spring and autumn would likely offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, with fewer crowds than peak summer.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to hike the Circuit des lacs — loop from Troncens. It is a publicly accessible trail.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like picnic areas along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, for your convenience, picnic tables are available by Lac de Monpardiac, offering a pleasant spot for a break during your hike.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the PR de Troncens, Chemin de Jacotte, Chemin du Lac, Chemin du Bois du Mède, and Chemin de Hustaraille.

faqs.question: Are there maps available for the Circuit des lacs — loop from Troncens?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is well-documented, and free hiking maps are often available, guiding hikers through its diverse scenery and nestling lakes.
